revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:حدث فلكي]][[bn:জ্যোতির্বৈজ্ঞানিক প্রপঞ্চ]][[de:Astronomisches Ereignis]][[el:Ουράνιο φαινόμενο]][[fa:رویداد آسمانی]][[ja:天文現象]][[lb:Astronomescht Ereegnes]][[ms:Peristiwa cakerawala]][[no:Astronomisk begivenhet]][[pl:Zjawisko astronomiczne]][[vi:Sự kiện thiên thể]][[zh:天文現象]]


{{Original research|date=January 2016|qq=}}
{{Ruby|'''Astronomical Phenomena'''|てんもんげんしょう|qq=}} refer to various {{ill|Phenomenon|en|Phenomenon|qid=Q483247|lt=phenomena|1=Phenomenon}} that occur in the heavens (this term encompasses the {{ill|Sky|en|Sky|qid=Q527|lt=sky|1=Sky}}, the upper layers of the {{ill|Atmosphere|en|Atmosphere|qid=Q8104|lt=atmosphere|1=Atmosphere}}, and even {{ill|Outer space|en|Outer space|qid=Q4169|lt=outer space|1=Outer space}}). These phenomena were traditionally referred to as '''tenmon''' (literally, "heavenly patterns"), likening them to patterns or motifs, and their cyclical changes were studied for use in the creation of {{ill|Calendar|en|Calendar|qid=Q12132|lt=calendars|1=Calendar}} and {{ill|divination|ja|卜占|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/divination|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 占い|1=Divination|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1043197}}. The term originates from passages in the {{ill|I Ching|en|I Ching|qid=Q181937|lt=I Ching|1=I Ching}}, such as in the "Bi" hexagram: "Observing the patterns of the heavens to discern changes in time" and the Xici commentary: "Looking up to observe the heavenly patterns and down to study the earth, thereby understanding the causes of darkness and light."<ref>World Encyclopedia, 2nd Edition, "Tenmon."</ref> The term {{Ruby|'''Tenshō'''|てんしょう|qq=}} is also used synonymously.
These phenomena were originally the focus of {{ill|Weather lore|en|Weather lore|qid=Q252670|lt=sky watching|1=Weather lore}}, but later became distinct from {{ill|meteorology|ja|気象|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/meteorology|1=meteorological phenomenon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16332653}}, focusing primarily on {{ill|astronomical observation|ja|天体観測|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/astronomical observation|1=astronomical observation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11273602}}. The motion of {{ill|Planet|en|Planet|qid=Q634|lt=planets|1=Planet}} in particular was studied in detail by {{ill|Astronomer|en|Astronomer|qid=Q11063|lt=astronomers|1=Astronomer}} across both the East and the West. After the invention of the {{ill|Telescope|en|Telescope|qid=Q4213|lt=telescope|1=Telescope}}, celestial bodies beyond the {{ill|Sun|en|Sun|qid=Q525|lt=Sun|1=Sun}} and {{ill|Moon|en|Moon|qid=Q405|lt=Moon|1=Moon}} were clearly recognized as distinct {{ill|Astronomical object|en|Astronomical object|qid=Q6999|lt=astronomical objects|1=Astronomical object}}, leading to the development of astronomy as a branch of {{ill|Physics|en|Physics|qid=Q413|lt=physics|1=Physics}} (→{{ill|Astrophysics|en|Astrophysics|qid=Q37547|lt=astrophysics|1=Astrophysics}}). Since then, {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|qid=Q333|lt=astronomy|1=Astronomy}} has become a natural science that studies various phenomena in the universe, including {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=stars|1=Star}}.
On the other hand, the divination aspect of astronomical phenomena was stripped of academic credibility but continues to persist as part of modern {{ill|Faith|en|Faith|qid=Q5410500|lt=faith|1=Faith}} and {{ill|Superstition|en|Superstition|qid=Q133182|lt=superstition|1=Superstition}}. Modern astronomical observation is not only a professional activity but also serves as a popular {{ill|Recreation|en|Recreation|qid=Q184872|lt=recreational activity|1=Recreation}} (see {{ill|stargazing|ja|天体観望|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stargazing|1=stargazing|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442227}}).<ref>Examples include observatories offering accommodations for visitors, such as {{ill|Pic du Midi de Bigorre|en|Pic du Midi de Bigorre|qid=Q1139060|lt=Pic du Midi|1=Pic du Midi de Bigorre}} ([http://www.picdumidi.com/nuits-au-sommet/ Pic du Midi official site] and [http://guide.travel.co.jp/article/11182/]). Similarly, accommodations with observatories are becoming increasingly common.</ref>
== Examples of Astronomical Phenomena ==
=== Phenomena caused by Solar System bodies ===
* {{ill|Eclipse|en|Eclipse|qid=Q141022|lt=Eclipses|1=Eclipse}} and {{ill|Occultation|en|Occultation|qid=Q202746|lt=occultations|1=Occultation}} (e.g., {{ill|Lunar eclipse|en|Lunar eclipse|qid=Q44235|lt=lunar eclipses|1=Lunar eclipse}}, {{ill|Solar eclipse|en|Solar eclipse|qid=Q3887|lt=solar eclipses|1=Solar eclipse}}, {{ill|stellar occultations|ja|星食|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stellar occultations|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 掩蔽|1=Occultation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q202746}}, {{ill|transits|ja|日面通過|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/transits|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 太陽面通過|1=Solar transit|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7556892}}) – Eclipses were often regarded as ominous signs.
* {{ill|Comet|en|Comet|qid=Q3559|lt=Comets|1=Comet}}
* Changes in celestial positions:
** {{ill|Opposition (astronomy)|en|Opposition (astronomy)|qid=Q105562|lt=Opposition|1=Opposition (astronomy)}}
** {{ill|Conjunction (astronomy)|en|Conjunction (astronomy)|qid=Q191536|lt=Conjunctions|1=Conjunction (astronomy)}}
*** {{ill|Inferior conjunction|ja|内合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inferior conjunction|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合 (天文)|1=Conjunction (astronomy)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q191536}}, {{ill|Superior conjunction|ja|外合|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Superior conjunction|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 合 (天文)|1=Conjunction (astronomy)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q191536}}
** {{ill|Stationary points|ja|留|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stationary points|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 順行・逆行|1=Retrograde and prograde motion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q312021}}
** {{ill|Greatest elongations|ja|最大離角|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greatest elongations|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 離角|1=Elongation (astronomy)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q271439}} (e.g., eastern and western greatest elongations)
** {{ill|Quadrature|ja|矩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Quadrature|1=Q11584673|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584673}} (e.g., eastern and western quadrature)
** Phases of the Moon and inner planets
=== Phenomena caused by extrasolar celestial bodies ===
* {{ill|Variable star|en|Variable star|qid=Q6243|lt=Variable stars|1=Variable star}}
* {{ill|Nova|en|Nova|qid=Q6458|lt=Novae|1=Nova}} – Referred to as {{ill|Guest star (astronomy)|en|Guest star (astronomy)|qid=Q31759|lt=guest stars|1=Guest star (astronomy)}} in ancient China.
** {{ill|Gamma-ray burst|en|Gamma-ray burst|qid=Q22247|lt=Gamma-ray bursts|1=Gamma-ray burst}}
* {{ill|Gravitational lens|en|Gravitational lens|qid=Q185243|lt=Gravitational lensing|1=Gravitational lens}}
== References ==
<references />

== See Also ==
* {{ill|Astronomical object|en|Astronomical object|qid=Q6999|lt=Astronomical objects|1=Astronomical object}}
** {{ill|Solar System|en|Solar System|qid=Q544|lt=Solar System|1=Solar System}}
** {{ill|Sun|en|Sun|qid=Q525|lt=Sun|1=Sun}}
** {{ill|Earth|en|Earth|qid=Q2|lt=Earth|1=Earth}}
** {{ill|Moon|en|Moon|qid=Q405|lt=Moon|1=Moon}}
* {{ill|Astronomical observation|ja|天体観測|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomical observation|1=astronomical observation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11273602}}
* {{ill|Tenmondō|ja|天文道|1=Tenmondō (astrology)|lt=Tenmondō|qq= (astrology)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmondō (astrology)|qq=}}
* {{ill|Astronomy|en|Astronomy|qid=Q333|lt=Astronomy|1=Astronomy}}
* {{ill|Chronology of astronomical events|ja|天文現象の年表|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chronology of astronomical events|1=Q11442661|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11442661}}
{{Normdaten|qq=}}

{{translated page|ja|天文現象|version=98446428|comment=Imported full ja history}}
